assumptions: Match engine: independent Poisson goals; each team's expected goals set by the Elo-rating gap (host nations +50 Elo).
Top teams' strength is fitted to the betting market's title odds (Kalshi via Covers, 13 Jun 2026); the rest of the field is set by world-football Elo (15 Jun 2026).

Second calibration input: any scheduled but unplayed group game that has a betting line in match_odds.json is priced directly off the market's 1X2 (home/draw/away) odds — the model solves the Poisson scoring rates that reproduce that line — instead of inferring the game from the Elo gap.
Completed results so far are locked in; all remaining group games and every knockout tie are simulated.
Knockout ties level after 90' are resolved by an Elo-logistic extra-time/penalty model.
Best-8 third-placed teams allocated to bracket slots using FIFA's exact Annex C table (495 combinations).
Probabilities from 40,000 Monte Carlo tournament simulations; sampling error on a 50% probability is about ±0.3%.
A few lower-tier teams' Elo values are estimated (flagged in data); they barely affect the knockout picture and refine as results post.

matchups.why: Belgium and Senegal replace Egypt and South Korea as the projected pairing — driven by New Zealand 1-5 Belgium (Group G).
Ghana and Portugal replace Colombia and Croatia as the projected pairing — driven by a re-priced Croatia–Ghana betting line.
Iran replaces Algeria as Switzerland's projected opponent — driven by Egypt 1-1 Iran (Group G) and a re-priced Algeria–Austria betting line.
Colombia replaces Portugal as Ghana's projected opponent — driven by a re-priced Colombia–Portugal betting line.
Egypt replaces Belgium as Australia's projected opponent — driven by New Zealand 1-5 Belgium (Group G).
Belgium replaces Egypt as USA's projected opponent — driven by New Zealand 1-5 Belgium (Group G).

changes.summary: This rebuild ingested 4 new results (Uruguay 0-1 Spain (Group H); Cape Verde 0-0 Saudi Arabia (Group H); New Zealand 1-5 Belgium (Group G)…), a betting-market refresh that moved 3 teams' title odds (Argentina ▲; Netherlands ▼, Spain ▼) and 6 re-priced game lines. 6 bracket boxes now show a different pairing — see each for what moved it.
